meta.description: Generalized workflow engine — combined design spec and server implementation plan. Any domain entity can spawn a workflow instance. The engine knows steps, conditions, and advancement — never domain semantics.
meta.principle: The engine doesn't know what a 'waiver' is. It knows 'context field waiver_count is an integer that can be compared.' All domain knowledge lives in workflow definitions and the systems that populate context.
changelog: Merged flags into context — context_schema gains display:'toggle' annotation; flags column/struct/engine-fn removed; conditions drop flags. prefix; fixes latent normalization bug where toggle_flag did not trigger normalize()
Removed position from StepDefinition — array index is canonical order; resolves spec/plan contradiction
Specified normalization overflow — explicit NormalizationOverflow error; publish-time validation rejects >10 consecutive auto steps
Added completed_by and cancelled_by varchar(64) fields to workflow_instance
Added step_id uniqueness to publish-time validation checks
Added StepNotFound structured engine error for corrupt/missing step references
Added integer to context types in spec (aligns spec with plan)
Kept owner_type/owner_id on definitions (reversed finding — earns its place for direct lookup queries)

workflow_definition.description: A versioned, reusable pipeline template. Defines ordered steps and expected context shape. Owned by whatever domain concept needs a pipeline (product, department, asset type).

fields.name: string (e.g. 'Standard Paintball Flow', 'Marker Maintenance')
fields.context_schema: object — declares the data shape this workflow expects at runtime, including toggle-display fields that replace the old flags concept
fields.steps: ordered array of step definitions — array index is canonical progression order

versioning.description: Workflow definitions are versioned to prevent drift between definitions and running instances.
rules: Drafts are freely editable. Not available for instance creation.
Publishing creates an immutable version for breaking changes (steps, order, context_schema, conditions).
Non-breaking edits allowed on published versions: label text, phase names, UI hints.
Archiving a version prevents new instances but does not affect running instances pinned to it.
New instances default to the latest published version.

breaking_vs_non_breaking.breaking: Adding/removing/reordering steps, changing step types, modifying conditions, changing context_schema fields
breaking_vs_non_breaking.non_breaking: Editing labels, phase names, step descriptions, UI hints

condition_validation.description: All gate and auto conditions are validated at publish time, not runtime.
rules: On publish, every condition expression is parsed and validated against the definition's context_schema.
A condition referencing a field not in context_schema is a publish error.
A condition comparing incompatible types (e.g. number == boolean) is a publish error.
The parsed AST is stored alongside the condition string for runtime evaluation. No string parsing at execution time.

context_schema.description: Declares what runtime data this workflow expects. The engine validates instance context against this schema. Gate and auto conditions can only reference declared fields. Fields with display:'toggle' replace the old flags concept — they are boolean context fields rendered as independent toggles in the UI.

field_definition.display: string | null (optional — 'toggle' causes UI to render as independent toggle indicator instead of data field)

context_schema.notes: The engine treats context as a typed bag. It doesn't interpret values — it only evaluates conditions against them. Domain systems are responsible for populating and updating context fields on the workflow instance. Toggle-display fields are functionally identical to any other boolean context field — the annotation is purely a UI hint.

fields.step_id: string (stable identity — never changes even if step is reordered)
fields.label: string (human-readable)
fields.phase: string (UI grouping hint — opaque to engine)
fields.optional: boolean (default false — if true, can be skipped by operator)

identity_rules: step_id is the stable identity. Instances track current_step_id, not array index.
Array index is the canonical progression order. advance_to_next uses index + 1.
step_id is assigned at creation and never reused within a workflow definition.
Renaming a step preserves its step_id. Moving a step changes its array index but not its step_id.
step_id uniqueness within a definition is validated at publish time.

manual.description: Operator commands advancement. No conditions evaluated. 'This is done, move on.'

gate.description: Operator commands advancement. Engine evaluates condition against instance context. Blocks if condition is false.

config.condition_ast: object (parsed at publish time, used at runtime)
config.message: string (shown to operator when blocked, supports {field} interpolation)

gate.ui_hint: Advance button, red block indicator when condition fails

auto.description: Engine monitors trigger. Advances automatically when met. No operator action needed.

config.condition_ast: object (parsed at publish time, if trigger=condition)

auto.ui_hint: Timer or progress indicator, no advance button

workflow_instance.description: A single execution of a workflow definition version. Created by a domain entity, carries runtime context, tracks position in pipeline by step_id. The engine operates on instances — it never touches the domain entity directly.

fields.workflow_id: string (which definition row this runs — pins to the specific version)
fields.current_step_id: string (step_id of current position — stable across reorders)
fields.context: object (runtime data bag, validated against workflow's context_schema — includes toggle fields that were formerly flags)
fields.due_at: timestamp | null (for auto-duration steps: entered_step_at + duration_minutes)
fields.remaining_seconds: number | null (set on pause for duration steps, used to recompute due_at on resume)
fields.created_by: string (operator or system that spawned the instance)
fields.completed_by: string | null (operator or 'system' that completed or cancelled the instance)
fields.cancelled_by: string | null (operator who cancelled — null if not cancelled)
fields.split_from: string | null (instance_id if this was split from another)

status_semantics.active: Running through the pipeline. Advancement permitted. Auto-monitor watches this instance.
status_semantics.paused: Temporarily halted. No advancement. Auto-monitor ignores. Duration timers frozen (remaining_seconds preserved).
status_semantics.completed: Reached final step. Terminal. Immutable. completed_by records actor.
status_semantics.cancelled: Terminated before completion. Terminal. cancelled_by records actor.
status_semantics.split: Original instance after a split operation. Terminal. Children tracked via split_from.

workflow_instance.notes: The instance does not know what created it. A check-in group holds a workflow_instance_id. A maintenance ticket holds a workflow_instance_id. The engine doesn't care — it sees instances.

advancement_engine.description: Stateless logic that evaluates step type against instance context and determines whether advancement is permitted.
advancement_engine.core_insight: Three concepts: Definition (immutable blueprint), Instance (mutable runtime state), Engine (pure functions: (definition, instance) → Mutation). The StepType enum IS the advancement logic. The Mutation struct describes what changed. The engine has zero side effects, zero database access, zero awareness of HTTP or concurrency.

preconditions: Parent entity must not be locked

behavior.manual: Advance to next step by array index (index + 1). Update current_step_id, entered_step_at. If next step is auto-duration, compute and set due_at.
behavior.gate: Evaluate condition_ast against instance context. If true, advance (same as manual). If false, return block message with interpolated context values.
behavior.auto: Reject — auto steps are not operator-advanceable. Engine monitors these independently.

advance.on_last_step: Set instance status to 'completed', set completed_at, set completed_by.

skip.behavior: Marks step as skipped, advances to next step by array index. Updates entered_step_at.

set_position.behavior: Operator override. Sets current_step_id to any step. Updates entered_step_at. If target is auto-duration, computes due_at from now. Used for late arrivals, corrections, split groups.

update_context.behavior: Merge fields into instance context. Validates against context_schema (type checking). Triggers re-evaluation via normalize() — handles both toggle-display fields and step-driving data uniformly.
update_context.notes: This is how domain systems feed data into the engine. A waiver service calls update_context({ waiver_count: 12 }). An operator taps a toggle, calling update_context({ photos_taken: true }). The engine doesn't know what either means.

pause.behavior: Set status to 'paused'. If on auto-duration step, compute remaining_seconds from due_at - now, clear due_at. Auto-monitor stops watching.

resume.behavior: Set status to 'active'. If on auto-duration step, recompute due_at = now + remaining_seconds, clear remaining_seconds. Auto-monitor resumes watching.

cancel.behavior: Set status to 'cancelled'. Set completed_at. Set cancelled_by to actor. Terminal.

normalization.description: After any step transition (advance, skip, set_position, resume, auto_advance, update_context), the service layer calls Engine::normalize(). This prevents an instance from resting on a satisfied auto-condition step or an auto-duration step with 0 minutes.
normalization.behavior: Loop: while current step is auto and trigger is satisfied, advance to next step. Safety cap: 10 transitions.

overflow.on_cap_hit: Return NormalizationOverflow error. The mutation is NOT applied. The definition is broken — 10+ consecutive auto steps that all fire is a design error.
overflow.publish_time_guard: Publish-time validation rejects definitions with >10 consecutive auto steps to prevent this scenario.

auto_monitoring.description: Background process that watches active instances on auto steps.

duration_trigger.mechanism: Poll instances where status = 'active' and due_at is not null and due_at <= now.
duration_trigger.on_trigger: Auto-advance to next step. Update current_step_id, entered_step_at. If next step is also auto-duration, compute new due_at. Set completed_by to 'system' if instance completes.
duration_trigger.resilience: due_at is persisted. Server restart does not lose timers. Poll picks up where it left off.

condition_trigger.mechanism: Re-evaluated whenever update_context is called on an instance that is active and on an auto-condition step — handled inline via normalize(), not by the monitor.
condition_trigger.on_trigger: If condition_ast evaluates to true, auto-advance to next step.

errors.StepNotFound: Returned when current_step_id does not match any step_id in the definition. Structured error with the missing step_id. Prevents panics on corrupt or manually-edited data.
errors.NormalizationOverflow: Returned when normalize() hits the 10-transition safety cap. Mutation is not applied. Indicates a broken definition.
errors.Blocked: Returned on gate advancement when condition evaluates false. Includes interpolated message and structured MissingField reason when applicable.
errors.Rejected: Returned when attempting to operator-advance an auto step.

concurrency.description: The workflow engine does not implement its own concurrency control. It inherits concurrency from the parent entity that owns the workflow instance.
concurrency.mechanism: Parent entities (bookings, maintenance tickets, rental orders) provide epoch-based optimistic locking and pessimistic locks for destructive operations.

contract.all_mutating_operations: Require epoch from the parent entity. Server checks epoch match and lock status before processing.
contract.epoch_increment: Every workflow mutation (advance, skip, context update, pause, resume, cancel, split) increments the parent entity's epoch.
contract.lock_respect: If parent entity is locked, all workflow mutations are rejected with lock holder info.

concurrency.why_not_instance_level: Epoch lives on the parent, not the instance. A waiver submission on one child group increments the parent epoch, causing an operator advancing a sibling group to refresh. This catches cross-instance conflicts within the same parent — broader protection than instance-level versioning.
concurrency.generalization: Any domain entity that spawns workflow instances must implement the epoch/lock pattern. The workflow engine assumes it exists.

split_mechanics.description: Splits a workflow instance into two. Workflow-agnostic — operates on instances regardless of which pipeline they run.

behavior.creates: Two new instances from the same workflow_id (same definition version row)
behavior.position: Both start at the original's current_step_id (or operator sets different step_ids)
behavior.context: Caller provides context for each (e.g. headcount split). Engine validates against context_schema. Toggle-display fields are part of context and are included in the split.
behavior.original: Original instance status set to 'split'. Terminal.
behavior.tracking: Both new instances have split_from set to original instance_id
behavior.timers: If original was on auto-duration step, both children get fresh due_at computed from now.

split_mechanics.atomicity: InnoDB transaction with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on original. Two child INSERTs, UPDATE original to 'split', epoch increment. All or nothing.
split_mechanics.notes: The split mechanic doesn't know about headcount, waivers, or bookings. It knows about context bags and step_ids. The calling domain system (check-in service) is responsible for splitting context correctly.

condition_language.description: Simple expression language for gate and auto conditions. Intentionally limited. Evaluated against instance context. Parsed and validated at publish time — never parsed at runtime.

grammar.field: any key declared in context_schema (including toggle-display fields — no special prefix needed)
grammar.value: integer | number | boolean | string literal | field reference

checks: All referenced fields exist in context_schema
Operator is valid for the field's type (no >= on booleans)
Field-to-field comparisons have compatible types (integer and number are compatible for comparison)
Expression parses into a valid AST
All step_ids in the definition are unique
No more than 10 consecutive auto steps in the definition
validation_at_publish.output: Stored condition_ast on the step config. Runtime evaluator uses AST, never re-parses the string.

constraints: No compound expressions (no AND/OR). One condition per gate. Use multiple sequential gates for compound logic.
No function calls. No arithmetic. If a derived value is needed, it should be a computed context field populated by the domain system.
If a condition can't be expressed in this language, it should be a manual step with operator judgment. The operator is the smart part of the system.

known_limitations.v1: String literals with spaces break the whitespace parser. Acceptable — all real conditions are numeric or boolean comparisons. Document the limitation.
known_limitations.v2_candidate: Add quoted string support if needed.

generic_rendering.description: Any UI that renders workflow instances does so generically. It reads the workflow definition and instance state — never domain-specific logic.

rendering_rules.progress: Steps displayed as ordered indicators (by array index) grouped by phase. Completed = filled, current = highlighted, upcoming = dim.

current_step_action.gate: Show advance button. On failure, show block message with interpolated context values.
current_step_action.auto_duration: Show countdown timer based on due_at. No advance button.
current_step_action.auto_condition: Show waiting indicator. No advance button.

rendering_rules.context_toggles: Context fields with display:'toggle' in the schema are rendered as independent toggle indicators alongside the progress indicator. Toggling calls update_context({ field: !current_value }).
rendering_rules.phases: Group steps visually. Collapse completed phases. Highlight active phase.
rendering_rules.optional_steps: Show skip action alongside advance button.
rendering_rules.paused_instance: Dim the progress indicator. Show 'Paused' status. If on duration step, show frozen time remaining.
rendering_rules.cancelled_instance: Grey out. Show 'Cancelled' status. No actions available.
rendering_rules.split_instance: Show as terminal with link to child instances.

generic_rendering.notes: Adding a new workflow with different steps, phases, and conditions requires zero UI code changes. The renderer reads definitions.

context_schema.description: Number of guests in the group
Number of signed waivers linked to this group

config.message: Waiver count ({waiver_count}) does not match headcount ({headcount})

context_schema.description: Number of guests in the group
Number of signed waivers linked to this group

config.message: Waiver count ({waiver_count}) does not match headcount ({headcount})

context_schema.description: Number of parts flagged for replacement during inspection
Chronograph reading in feet per second

config.message: Pressure {pressure_psi} PSI is below 800 PSI minimum
Velocity {chrono_fps} FPS exceeds 280 FPS safety limit

context_schema.description: Number of rental items issued to guest
Number of rental items returned by guest
Whether returned items have been inspected for damage

config.message: Returned items have not been inspected

domain_integration_pattern.description: How domain systems connect to the workflow engine without coupling.

pattern.1_create: Domain entity creates a workflow instance: POST /api/workflow-instances { workflow_key, initial_context }. Engine pins to latest published version.
pattern.2_link: Domain entity stores the returned instance_id on itself (e.g. check_in_group.workflow_instance_id, maintenance_ticket.workflow_instance_id)
pattern.3_feed: Domain systems update instance context as events occur: PATCH /api/workflow-instances/{id}/context { waiver_count: 12 }. All mutations include parent epoch. Toggle-display fields updated the same way: { photos_taken: true }.
pattern.4_advance: Operators advance via UI, engine evaluates: POST /api/workflow-instances/{id}/advance. Epoch and lock checked before processing.
pattern.5_render: UI reads instance + definition version, renders generically. Toggle-display fields identified by schema annotation.

domain_integration_pattern.concurrency_requirement: Any domain entity that spawns workflow instances must implement the epoch/lock pattern from concurrency.json. The workflow engine assumes the parent provides epoch on all mutations and lock status is checkable.

GET /{id}.description: Workflow definition with steps, context_schema, and version history

POST /{id}/publish.description: Validate conditions against context_schema, validate step_id uniqueness, validate <=10 consecutive auto steps, publish version, make immutable for breaking changes

POST /{id}/archive.description: Archive version (no new instances, running instances unaffected)

DELETE /{id}.description: Delete draft only. Published/archived versions cannot be deleted.

POST /.description: Create instance from latest published version with initial context

GET /{id}.description: Instance state: current_step_id, context, status, due_at

POST /{id}/advance.description: Attempt to advance current step. Requires epoch.

POST /{id}/skip.description: Skip current step (if optional, not gate). Requires epoch.

PATCH /{id}/context.description: Update instance context fields (including toggles). Requires epoch.

POST /{id}/split.description: Split instance into two with provided contexts. Requires epoch.

POST /{id}/pause.description: Pause instance. Freezes duration timers. Requires epoch.

POST /{id}/resume.description: Resume paused instance. Restarts duration timers. Requires epoch.

module_structure.description: Lives in the existing server/api crate. No new workspace crate — it depends on DatabaseConnection, AppState, Error, ApiResult, RouteLock, all of which live in api.

layout.server/api/src/enums/workflow.rs: All enums: DefinitionStatus, InstanceStatus, StepType, etc.
Condition AST, parser (~60 lines), evaluator (~40 lines)
layout.server/api/src/types/workflow/definition: WorkflowDefinition struct, step defs, DB queries
layout.server/api/src/types/workflow/engine.rs: Pure functions (THE engine). No db, no side effects.
layout.server/api/src/types/workflow/epoch_guar: Pre-mutation check: load parent, verify lock + epoch
layout.server/api/src/api/workflow_instances/: Instance mutation handlers (advance, skip, pause, etc.)

core_enums.description: All follow existing repr(u8) + from_u8() convention from campaign_status.rs.

methods: is_terminal() → true for Completed, Cancelled, Split

StepType.notes: #[serde(tag = "type")] for JSON storage. Config lives inside the variant — not in a separate struct. The enum IS the polymorphism.

variants: SchemaViolation { field: String, expected: String, got: String }

rows.name: draft
rows.description: Editable. Not available for instance creation.

rows.name: published
rows.description: Immutable for breaking changes. Available for instance creation.

rows.name: archived
rows.description: No new instances. Running instances unaffected.

rows.name: paused
rows.description: Halted. No advancement. Duration timers frozen.

columns.id: int PK auto_increment (row identity — FK target for instances)
columns.workflow_key: varchar(64) (stable identity across all versions of the same workflow)
columns.name: varchar(128)
columns.context_schema: mediumtext (JSON array of field defs — includes toggle-display fields)
columns.steps: mediumtext (JSON array of step defs — includes ASTs, no position field)
columns.updated_at: datetime DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

constraints: INDEX (workflow_key, status_id, version) — for 'latest published' query
INDEX (owner_type, owner_id) — for 'workflows for this entity' query
FK status_id → workflow_definition_status ON DELETE RESTRICT
workflow_definition.notes: workflow_key is the stable identity. id is the row PK used for FK from instances. Publishing creates a new row (new id, same workflow_key, incremented version). No flags column — toggle fields live in context_schema.

columns.workflow_id: int FK → workflow_definition (specific version row, pins the instance)
columns.completed_by: varchar(64) NULL (operator or 'system' that completed the instance)
columns.split_from: int NULL FK self-referential ON DELETE SET NULL
columns.parent_entity_type: varchar(64) (for monitor to find parent epoch)

constraints: FK workflow_id → workflow_definition ON DELETE RESTRICT
FK status_id → workflow_instance_status ON DELETE RESTRICT
FK split_from → workflow_instance ON DELETE SET NULL
workflow_instance.notes: No epoch column — epoch lives on the parent entity. No flags column — toggle values live in context.

database_schema.migration_sql:
SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS = 0;

CREATE TABLE `workflow_definition_status` (
  `id` tinyint NOT NULL,
  `name` varchar(32) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `description` varchar(255) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_general_ci;

INSERT INTO `workflow_definition_status` (`id`, `name`, `description`) VALUES
  (0, 'draft',     'Editable. Not available for instance creation.'),
  (1, 'published', 'Immutable for breaking changes. Available for instance creation.'),
  (2, 'archived',  'No new instances. Running instances unaffected.');

CREATE TABLE `workflow_instance_status` (
  `id` tinyint NOT NULL,
  `name` varchar(32) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `description` varchar(255) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_general_ci;

INSERT INTO `workflow_instance_status` (`id`, `name`, `description`) VALUES
  (0, 'active',    'Running. Advancement permitted. Auto-monitor watches.'),
  (1, 'paused',    'Halted. No advancement. Duration timers frozen.'),
  (2, 'completed', 'Reached final step. Terminal.'),
  (3, 'cancelled', 'Terminated before completion. Terminal.'),
  (4, 'split',     'Original instance after split. Terminal.');

CREATE TABLE `workflow_definition` (
  `id` int NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `workflow_key` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `name` varchar(128) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `owner_type` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `owner_id` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `version` int NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  `status_id` tinyint NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `published_at` datetime DEFAULT NULL,
  `context_schema` mediumtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `steps` mediumtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `created_at` datetime NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  `updated_at` datetime NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
  UNIQUE KEY `uq_workflow_key_version` (`workflow_key`, `version`),
  KEY `ix_workflow_def_latest` (`workflow_key`, `status_id`, `version`),
  KEY `ix_workflow_def_owner` (`owner_type`, `owner_id`),
  CONSTRAINT `fk_workflow_def_status` FOREIGN KEY (`status_id`)
    REFERENCES `workflow_definition_status` (`id`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_general_ci;

CREATE TABLE `workflow_instance` (
  `id` int NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `workflow_id` int NOT NULL,
  `current_step_id` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `context` mediumtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `status_id` tinyint NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `entered_step_at` datetime NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  `due_at` datetime DEFAULT NULL,
  `remaining_seconds` int DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_at` datetime NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  `completed_at` datetime DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_by` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `completed_by` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci DEFAULT NULL,
  `cancelled_by` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci DEFAULT NULL,
  `split_from` int DEFAULT NULL,
  `parent_entity_type` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  `parent_entity_id` varchar(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_general_ci NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
  KEY `ix_instance_workflow` (`workflow_id`),
  KEY `ix_instance_status` (`status_id`),
  KEY `ix_instance_auto_poll` (`status_id`, `due_at`),
  KEY `ix_instance_split_from` (`split_from`),
  KEY `ix_instance_parent` (`parent_entity_type`, `parent_entity_id`),
  CONSTRAINT `fk_instance_workflow` FOREIGN KEY (`workflow_id`)
    REFERENCES `workflow_definition` (`id`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  CONSTRAINT `fk_instance_status` FOREIGN KEY (`status_id`)
    REFERENCES `workflow_instance_status` (`id`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  CONSTRAINT `fk_instance_split_from` FOREIGN KEY (`split_from`)
    REFERENCES `workflow_instance` (`id`) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_general_ci;

SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS = 1;

steps_mediumtext.description: Serialized Vec<StepDefinition> — no position field, array index is order

example.label: Waivers Complete
example.message: Waiver count ({waiver_count}) does not match headcount ({headcount})

context_mediumtext_on_instance.description: Serialized HashMap<String, ContextValue> — includes toggle values

mutation_struct.description: All-Option fields describing what changed. apply_mutation() builds a dynamic UPDATE from non-None fields.
mutation_struct.notes: current_step_position is NOT stored — derive it at read time by looking up current_step_id in the definition's steps array index.

engine_functions.description: engine.rs contains a zero-field Engine struct used as a namespace. Every function takes &WorkflowDefinition and/or &WorkflowInstance and returns Result<(AdvanceResult, Mutation), WorkflowError>.

advance.logic: Match on StepType: Manual→next, Gate→eval then next or block, Auto→reject. Returns StepNotFound if current_step_id not in definition.

skip.logic: Check optional + not gate, then next. Returns StepNotFound if current_step_id not in definition.

set_position.logic: Set current_step_id, compute due_at if auto-duration. Returns StepNotFound if target_step_id not in definition.

update_context.logic: Merge fields into context. Validate types against context_schema. Toggle-display and data fields go through the same path.

auto_advance.logic: Same as advance_to_next but callable by monitor. Sets completed_by to 'system' if instance completes.

normalize.logic: Loop: while current step is auto and trigger is satisfied, advance. Safety cap: 10 transitions. On cap hit: return NormalizationOverflow error — mutation is NOT applied.

internal_helpers.advance_to_next: Finds next step by array index from current step_id (index + 1). If no next step → Completed. If next step is auto-duration → compute due_at.
internal_helpers.find_step_index: Looks up step_id in definition's steps array. Returns StepNotFound error if not found.

engine_functions.missing_field_semantics: If a condition references a context field that hasn't been populated yet, the condition evaluates to false (gate blocks, auto-condition does not advance). The AdvanceResult::Blocked message includes MissingField('field_name') as a structured reason.
engine_functions.message_interpolation: {field} placeholders in gate block messages replaced with context values. Missing fields render as {field_name} unchanged. ~15 lines.

service_layer.description: Follows existing patterns. No controller struct needed — workflow operations are stateless request-response.

key_methods.WorkflowInstance::load_with_definition(i: Loads instance + its pinned definition version
key_methods.WorkflowInstance::apply_mutation(mutatio: Dynamic UPDATE from Mutation non-None fields
key_methods.WorkflowInstance::due_instances(db): Auto-monitor query: WHERE status_id=0 AND due_at IS NOT NULL AND due_at <= NOW()
key_methods.WorkflowDefinition::latest_published(wor: For instance creation. WHERE workflow_key=? AND status_id=1 ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1
key_methods.WorkflowDefinition::publish(id, db): Parse + validate all conditions, validate step_id uniqueness, validate <=10 consecutive auto steps, store ASTs, flip status

epoch_guard.check: load parent, check lock timeout, check epoch match
epoch_guard.parent_type_validation: Validated against a known set (enum with from_str) to prevent SQL injection via table name

api_handlers.description: Follow existing struct-based pattern from secrets_post.rs. Empty struct, static logic() method. 7 definition endpoints + 10 instance endpoints (was 11 — toggle_flag removed).
handler_pattern: 4. If mutation changed current_step_id → call Engine::normalize() and merge resulting mutation

auto_monitor.description: Follows ScanController/ScanSweeper pattern. Spawned from main.rs via WorkflowMonitor::run().
auto_monitor.query: WHERE status_id=0 AND due_at IS NOT NULL AND due_at <= NOW()
per_result_flow: Call epoch_guard (skip if locked — respects parent locks)
Persist mutation (sets completed_by to 'system' if instance completes)
auto_monitor.resilience: due_at is persisted — survives server restart. Poll picks up where it left off.
auto_monitor.condition_triggers: Not handled by monitor. Evaluated inline inside update_context handler via normalize() — when context changes satisfy the condition, auto-advance happens immediately.

implementation_sequence: 1. Migration file — lookup tables + core tables (no flags columns, with completed_by/cancelled_by)
2. enums/workflow.rs — all enums (no FlagDefinition, includes WorkflowError with StepNotFound/NormalizationOverflow)
3. types/workflow/context.rs — ContextValue (with Integer), ContextSchema (with display annotation), validation
4. types/workflow/condition.rs — parser, evaluator, validation (no flags. prefix handling)
5. types/workflow/definition.rs — struct (no flags field, no position in steps), DB queries
6. types/workflow/instance.rs — struct (no flags column, with completed_by/cancelled_by), DB queries, apply_mutation
7. types/workflow/engine.rs — pure functions (no toggle_flag, normalize with NormalizationOverflow, StepNotFound on all step lookups)
8. Unit tests for engine — every branch against synthetic defs/instances
10. api/workflows/ — definition CRUD handlers (publish validates step_id uniqueness + consecutive auto cap)
11. api/workflow_instances/ — 10 instance mutation handlers (no flags endpoint)
12. types/workflow/monitor.rs — auto-step poller (sets completed_by on completion)
14. Add WorkflowError variants to Error enum with #[from]
15. Add Resource::Workflows to permission enum for RouteLock
files_to_modify: server/api/src/types/route_collection.rs — add workflows and workflow_instances scopes in v1()
server/api/src/main.rs — add WorkflowMonitor::run(&shared_data).await; alongside other sweepers
server/api/src/types/permissions.rs — add Resource::Workflows variant to permission enum

unit_tests_engine: Gate block (condition false) — with interpolated message
Gate block (missing field) — with MissingField structured reason
Auto step rejected on operator advance
Resume — timer math (due_at recomputed from remaining_seconds)
Normalize safety cap hit — NormalizationOverflow returned
unit_tests_condition: Parse valid expressions (field == field, field >= literal, field == boolean)
Validate against schema — missing field rejected
Validate against schema — type mismatch rejected (>= on boolean)
Evaluate against context — integer equality (exact match)
unit_tests_publish_validation: Condition referencing non-existent context field rejected
integration_tests: Create definition → publish → create instance → advance through full pipeline → verify completed status + completed_by
Monitor test: create instance on auto-duration step with 0-minute duration → poll → verify auto-advanced + completed_by = 'system'
Split test: create instance → split → verify original is 'split' status, two children exist with correct context (including toggle values)
Normalization test: definition with consecutive auto-condition steps where conditions are pre-satisfied → advance into first → verify instance lands on the next manual/gate step
Cancel test: cancel active instance → verify cancelled_by set correctly

reference_patterns.description: Existing files to follow. All paths relative to server/api/src/. Implementation happens in the server worktree.

repr_u8_enum.copy: Enum shape, bounds-check test, serde derive

handler_struct.copy: Empty struct, static async method, WereChecked + Json<T> + Data<AppState> params

red_team.description: All findings from original plan red team + fresh-eyes review, with resolutions.

original_plan_findings.resolution: Resolved: ContextValue::Integer(i64) added. Counts use Integer with strict equality. Number(f64) reserved for measurements. No epsilon needed.
Epoch changes provide basic 'something changed' signal. Mutation struct can be serialized to append-only log in v2 with zero engine changes.

original_plan_findings.finding: String literals with spaces break condition parser
original_plan_findings.resolution: All real conditions are numeric/boolean. Documented limitation. Add quoted string support in v2.

original_plan_findings.finding: parent_type string as table name — SQL injection risk
original_plan_findings.resolution: Validate against ParentType enum with from_str(). Unknown types rejected before SQL construction.
Resolved: workflow_key added as stable identity. Unique constraint on (workflow_key, version). Instance FK pins to specific row id.
normalize() loop after every step transition. Auto steps that are immediately satisfiable fire without waiting. Safety cap of 10 transitions.
Missing field → condition evaluates false. Structured MissingField reason in block message.
Monitor calls epoch_guard::check(). Locked parents skipped until next poll cycle.
Timer is operational signaling, not guest-facing. Client renders countdown from due_at. Server eventually advances.
InnoDB transaction with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE. All or nothing.

fresh_eyes_findings.finding: Merge flags into context with schema annotation
fresh_eyes_findings.verdict: STRENGTHENED — also fixes latent normalization bug where toggle_flag did not trigger normalize()
fresh_eyes_findings.action: context_schema gains display:'toggle' annotation. flags column/struct/engine-fn removed. Conditions drop flags. prefix.

fresh_eyes_findings.finding: Remove position from StepDefinition, use array index
fresh_eyes_findings.verdict: HOLDS — contradiction is real, array index is sufficient
fresh_eyes_findings.action: position removed from step definition. Array index is canonical order. advance_to_next uses index + 1.

fresh_eyes_findings.verdict: HOLDS — error is the correct behavior for a broken definition
fresh_eyes_findings.action: NormalizationOverflow error added. Mutation NOT applied on cap hit. Publish-time validation rejects >10 consecutive auto steps.

fresh_eyes_findings.verdict: HOLDS — note audit log overlap, these become denormalized shortcuts when audit log ships
fresh_eyes_findings.action: completed_by varchar(64) NULL and cancelled_by varchar(64) NULL added to workflow_instance.

fresh_eyes_findings.finding: Validate step_id uniqueness at publish time
fresh_eyes_findings.verdict: HOLDS — obvious correctness check, trivial to implement
HOLDS — cheap insurance, 3 lines of code
fresh_eyes_findings.action: StepNotFound added to WorkflowError. All step lookups in engine return this instead of panicking.

fresh_eyes_findings.finding: Update spec to include integer context type
fresh_eyes_findings.action: context_schema field types are now integer | number | boolean | string.

fresh_eyes_findings.verdict: REVERSED — owner_type/owner_id earns its place for direct lookup queries, consistent with other opaque type/id patterns

stress_test_summary.conclusion: The engine handles linear, forward-moving operational pipelines extremely well. Parallel branches require multiple instances + domain orchestration. Cycles use set_position. This is the right trade-off — trying to add DAG or cycle support would destroy the e=mc² simplicity.

future_considerations.priority: P1 — implement after core engine is proven

audit_event_log.description: Append-only log of state-changing operations on workflow instances.
audit_event_log.notes: Epoch changes on the parent entity provide basic audit for v1. completed_by/cancelled_by provide P0 terminal-state attribution. Formal event log adds granular step-level tracking, time-in-step analytics, and liability trail. When shipped, completed_by/cancelled_by become denormalized shortcuts.

idempotency_keys.description: Optional Idempotency-Key header on mutating endpoints to make retries safe.

richer_condition_language.description: Simple AND support (no parentheses) and 'in' operator. No functions or arithmetic. Quoted string literals.

context_write_policy.description: Per-field write access control (operator | system | admin) to prevent accidental edits to critical fields.
